230,PullRequestEvent,2018-01-06 22:48:22+00:00,6662075,joke2k/faker,89607,fcurella,,,161395194,,Close #667. Allow past dates within a second,closed,False,671,### What does this changes

We test for the difference between `start_date` and `end_date` in `date_time_between`. If it's `<= 1`,  we generate a random number between `0.0` and `1.0` by using `random.random()` instead of using `random.randint()`
When the difference between `start_date` and `end_date` in `date_time_between` is <= 1 second, the call to `random.randomint` fails with a `ValueError`
`random.random()` won't raise `ValueError` when the difference is `<= 1`

382,PullRequestEvent,2016-03-25 16:41:13+00:00,18313451,dsoprea/PySvn,715626,dsoprea,,,35549928,,Handle missing logentry tags in CommonClient.log_default ,closed,False,13,Hi, thanks for working on this library!

It's possible for XML logentry tags to be missing child tags, such as msg. I've seen this in one of our organization's repositories, but it's also confirmed by the schema definition. In fact, all the child tags of logentry are optional
column2: see line 29 of subversion/svn/schema/log.rnc in the Subversion source code: http://svn.apache.org/viewvc/subversion/trunk/subversion/svn/schema/log.rnc?view=markup

column1: When this happens, log_default throws a KeyError when trying to access the tag from a dictionary it's built, and therefore stops yielding messages.
This change uses dict.get() instead of square brackets, so if a tag is missing, the value in the yielded namedtuple will be None.

Another project has a similar issue: http://www.redmine.org/issues/18754",https://api.github.com/repos/dsoprea/PySvn/issues/13,2016-03-25T16:41:11Z,2016-03-25T16:41:11Z,2016-03-25T16:41:11Z,2,8,3,1,[],[],[],[],dsoprea,715626,User,False,[],https://github.com/dsoprea/PySvn/pull/13.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/dsoprea/PySvn/pulls/13/commits

I added basic support for Japanese.

The sentence tokenization is based on a simple regexp tokenizer, while the word tokenization being quite tricky in Japanese relies on [tinysegmenter](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/tinysegmenter). I import the library lazily to avoid enforcing it on users not using Japanese.

Stemming not being as important in Japanese as it is in many European languages (no plural, no genders, very few forms for verbs), I simply ignored it for now. I will look deeper into it if necessary.
I found the stopwords list at
which seems to be a project developed at Kyoto University.
I only tried it a little, but the results seem quite good.

1179,PullRequestEvent,2017-07-25 22:00:25+00:00,1998014,Diaoul/subliminal,10238474,sharkykh,,,132389656,,Fix lxml parsing for Addic7ed,open,False,795,"Basically, after like, 2 hours of trying different things,
I've come to the conclusion that,
1. Addic7ed HTML markup is probably broken.
2. Parsing using lxml worked just fine up until version lxml 3.7.0 was released.
See [the last Travis job log for `PARSER=lxml` that passed](https://travis-ci.org/Diaoul/subliminal/jobs/169318251#L620).
Something changed in lxml that caused it to not be able to parse Addic7ed's (broken) html properly, only parsing the some of the site.
Since we can't control Addic7ed's HTML code, we need to find a way around it.
Using a regex pattern is probably the simplest and fastest way to go about this, and it seems to work.
What it does is strip down the HTML to only the `<td class=""version"">` tags and discarding everything else.
Note: There are two commits, this is intentional, in order to show my progress.
- The first commit has a slower method - checking if it failed, and if it did then re-parsing with the stripped content.
This makes parsing the page with lxml much slower, **to a point where html.parser is faster**.
- The second commit just simplifies everything, and strips down the HTML **either way**.
If the stripping down fails, it falls back to using the current method of BeautifulSoup-ing the whole response content.
This method is faster than both the first commit, and the current method, because BS4 only has to parse about half the HTML (`1.07 MB => 621 KB`).

This commit solves Issue #62 and updates Jupyter notebook examples to be compliant with the latest version. Some things to note:

- __Demos that used the plotters API were removed.__ Some examples demonstrate by showing how to do it with the factory, and by the `plotters` API. Since both of them are deprecated, I just removed the other and focused on the updated way to do it.
- __Add a .gitignore file to untrack .ipynb_checkpoints__ I think they're not needed so I have git ignore them.

- __Explicitly pass a parameter to `cmap` to avoid matplotlib deprecation warning__. This seems a bit weird for me. My environment has matplotlib 2.0.2 and of course scikit-plot 0.3.1. When I call `plot_roc_curve` or `plot_precision_curve`, I encounter a deprecation warning on the use of `spectral` or `spectral_r`.
from sklearn.datasets import load_digits as load_data
MatplotlibDeprecationWarning: The spectral and spectral_r colormap was deprecated in version 2.0. Use nipy_spectral and nipy_spectral_r instead.

Although I checked the code in the repo and it's actually supplying `nipy_spectral`. Hmmm, not sure if it's just an environment problem for me. Either way, I solve it by explicitly passing the cmap parameter instead.
I committed a lot (almost per file), I can rebase my commits after your suggestion/revisions/review.
Thank you so much! Sorry if this PR took me a long time, thesis happened. 😞

2587,PullRequestEvent,2017-04-25 21:16:50+00:00,4742675,jbittel/django-mama-cas,814673,jbittel,,,117534210,,Multiple attribute values in SAML responses,closed,False,41,"I'm not sure if it's defined in the protocol, but the reference CAS implementation (at least v4.0.0) allows multiple <AttributeValue> tags per <Attribute> in SAML responses. This is useful, for example, for a ""groups"" attribute, with a user belonging to an arbitrary number of groups.

As best as I could tell, you can't presently do this with MamaCAS. If you return a list from an attribute callback, you get an etree serialization error. This PR changes it so that if you return a list from an attribute callback, each element of the list is added as a separate value.

I'm not crazy about giving lists special treatment, but I couldn't come up with a more generic way to determine what the callback intends us to do with the value. You obviously can't just look for iterables, since that includes strings. I suppose you could catch the serialization exception and try iterating over the value instead, but that doesn't feel quite right either. I'm open to suggestions!
Barring suggestions, I don't think making lists a special case is **too** bad, though, because:
1. Lists currently break the response anyway.

2. It seems a natural-enough convention--at least, it's how I assumed it would work when I first experimented.",https://api.github.com/repos/jbittel/django-mama-cas/issues/41,2017-04-25T21:16:49Z,2017-04-25T21:16:49Z,2017-04-25T21:16:49Z,2,36,2,2,[],[],[],[],jbittel,814673,User,False,[],https://github.com/jbittel/django-mama-cas/pull/41.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/jbittel/django-mama-cas/pulls/41/commits

2625,PullRequestEvent,2015-07-09 18:23:29+00:00,7133153,omab/python-social-auth,91022,omab,,,38504889,,"Use official python-saml 2.1.3 release, remove now-unsupported setting",closed,False,657,"The SAML backend can now use the latest official release of python-saml, rather than a fork, since the release now includes the required changes. Version 2.1.3 is largely the same as the commit hash previously specified, with one notable difference being that the ""NameIDFormats"" setting change that I had proposed was rejected in favor of [a simpler fix](https://github.com/onelogin/python-saml/commit/3a5847be93fd7e3af695d791989825cc6c118808) that should meet the same goals.
* Uses the official release of python-saml instead of the fork

* Removes the now-unsupported `NameIDFormats` setting, which was unlikely to be needed by most people anyways. Those who do need to override the `NameIDFormat` can configure it using the `SOCIAL_AUTH_SAML_SP_EXTRA` dict, but that is unlikely to be a common requirement.

3516,PullRequestEvent,2017-08-12 19:12:45+00:00,34864402,apache/incubator-mxnet,568948,piiswrong,47359,apache,131733489,,cuda support for linalg-functions, restructuring of linalg interfaces",closed,False,7147,"Linear algebra operators are now restructured to following interfaces (and cuda support has been added):
- c_lapack_api.h : Header to integrate lapack functions on CPU
- linalg.h:  Tensor based interface to call linear algebra operators on gpu/cpu in a unified way
- la_op.cc/la_op.cu: Operators that expose these functions to a user.

The whole implementation of cpu/gpu handling is in linalg_impl.h. It turned out that there are various specialties when dealing with cuBlas/cuSolver for the different functionalities, so decided to hide this in a separate file and not clutter the interface (linalg.h) with all these details.

We are planning to add more functions in the future (and other people are planning this too). So hopefully we can ensure that this structure is used as much as possible for new additional linear algebra functionalities.
This PR requires PR 274 for mshadow as prerequisite.
The CUDA functions still have to be tested. Am in the process of doing so but would like to send the changes already now for review.

3966,PullRequestEvent,2017-02-28 05:21:56+00:00,648332,assimp/assimp,1615426,saveman71,265533,assimp,108260711,,Allow to enable importers in a whitelist manner,open,False,1191,"In the case when we want to build assimp with only a few importers (let's say less than 10), it would be shorter to specify the importers in a whitelist manner, instead of disabling every other importer but the 10 we want.

4422,PullRequestEvent,2017-12-01 00:29:44+00:00,3409513,twisted/klein,50002,wsanchez,716546,twisted,110505909,,Klein.url_for() for reverse URL mapping,closed,False,173,"I'd like to propose `Klein.url_for()` method for reverse URL mapping.
def url_for(self, request, endpoint, values = None, method = None, force_external = False, append_unknown = True)

`request` is the `Request` object. It is used to obtain correct hostname for external links. Other arguments are just like in Werkzeug's [`MapAdapter.build()`](http://werkzeug.pocoo.org/docs/0.11/routing/#werkzeug.routing.MapAdapter.build).
It can be used like this:
from klein import route, run, url_for
Actually, Klein already has `url_for()` method on `IKleinRequest` that is assumed to be used like this:

But this looks like a piece of confusing magic. How does request know about url-mapping? Why are we asking the request to do url mapping, not the app? And this is working by exploiting the fact that `Request` is `Componentized` that can be problematic as described in #31. Having `url_for()` method on `app` seems more reasonable.

- `request` argument to `url_for` is needed only if `force_external=True`. Most of the links are internal, so it might be more convenient to make `request` arg optional. May be have two methods: `url_for(endpoint, ...)` that always returns internal link and `external_url_for(request, endpoint, ...)` for external ones?

If overall concept will be accepted I will add a docstring and example in docs.",https://api.github.com/repos/twisted/klein/issues/173,2017-12-01T00:29:43Z,2017-12-01T00:29:43Z,2017-12-01T00:29:43Z,14,76,1,3,CONTRIBUTOR,[],[],[],wsanchez,50002,User,False,[],https://github.com/twisted/klein/pull/173.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/twisted/klein/pulls/173/commits

4465,PullRequestEvent,2016-04-11 18:41:52+00:00,4978045,Parsely/pykafka,482743,benauthor,779641,Parsely,66041670,,Handle exceptions in intepreter shutdown in RequestHandler,open,False,522,"Supersedes my weak attempt this AM at https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/pull/521...

I am seeing assorted errors with some frequency originating in the handler function within RequestHandler._start_thread when using Pykafka in a web application that is run multithreaded under Gunicorn.

I see a recent commit d2a7e4c, which handles a TypeError caused by the messy nature of working with daemon threads. This is cool, but doesn't go quite far enough -- as the whole universe is getting deconstructed around the daemon thread, errors can originate in fairly hard-to-predict ways in this kind of context.
This patch uses an approach yanked from py3k's threading module -- just check to see if `sys` still exists before raising.
It's not a critical problem as the Python process is already going down, but causes a ton of log noise, which obscures real problems and is a bummer. Here are some of my errors from production:
Exception in thread 18: pykafka.RequestHandler.worker for broker-18.kafka.prclt.net:9092 (most likely raised during interpreter shutdown):
Unhandled exception in thread started by <bound method Thread.__bootstrap of <Thread(1: pykafka.RequestHandler.worker for broker-1.kafka.prclt.net:9092, stopped daemon 140024375449344)>>

4943,PullRequestEvent,2015-08-14 10:59:49+00:00,24993138,google/brotli,6939968,anthrotype,1342004,google,42138528,,support building compiled wheels packages using Appveyor + Travis ,closed,False,136,"Here's my attempt at using CI systems to run automated builds of Brotli Python extension module.
You can see the latest build logs here:
Only Windows (via Appveyor) and OS X (via Travis) are supported here.
PyPI does not accept precompiled Linux wheels anyway: see http://pythonwheels.com/
On Windows, I test both 32 and 64-bit Python, 2.7.x and 3.4.x. The extension is compiled using MSVC 2010.
On OS X, I use the [MacPython/terrify](https://github.com/MacPython/terryfy) utilities in order to download and configure different python setups.

I could successfully compile Brotli on Apple's system Python (2.7.x), as well as on Homebrew Python 2.7.10 and 3.4.3. However, I was not able to build Brotli using official Python installers from Python.org. The latter are compiled using OS X 10.6 SDK and GCC 4.2, which don't support advanced C++0x features required by Brotli.
We could configure both `appveyor.yml` and `.travis.yml` to deploy the compiled wheels to some public wheelhouse (even directly upload to PyPI) whenever a new release is tagged.
Let me know what you think.

- [x] ~Skipped item~ This is a documentation fix
Previously, when a topic node is selected, only the topic node would be passed into the download file list, so that none of the resources under this topic node were downloaded.

Currently, if a topic node is selected, then all of its descendants will be downloaded. On the other hand, if a topic node is de-selected, then all of its descendants will be excluded from the download list.
I'm not sure if the number of queries here is the most optimized. Any suggestions appreciated!
1. Import content with a topic node selected, then all of its descendants will be imported
2. Import content with a topic node selected and a resource node under that topic node not selected, then all of the topic node's descendants except the one resource node will be imported

* Use old `url_name` behavior in route decorators [5915][gh5915]
For `list_route` and `detail_route` maintain the old behavior of `url_name`,
basing it on the `url_path` instead of the function name.
`read_only` fields will now **always** be excluded from writable fields.
Previously `read_only` fields with a `default` value would use the `default` for create and update operations.
In order to maintain the old behaviour you may need to pass the value of `read_only` fields when calling `save()` in
Alternatively you may override `save()` or `create()` or `update()` on the serialiser as appropriate.
Without an explicit `default`, `allow_null` implies a default of `null` for outgoing serialisation. Previously such
fields were being skipped when read-only or otherwise not required.
**Possible backwards compatibility break** if you were relying on such fields being excluded from the outgoing
representation. In order to restore the old behaviour you can override `data` to exclude the field when `None`.
* Refactor dynamic route generation and improve viewset action introspectibility. [5705][gh5705]
`ViewSet`s have been provided with new attributes and methods that allow
it to introspect its set of actions and the details of the current action.
* Merged `list_route` and `detail_route` into a single `action` decorator.
* Get all extra actions on a `ViewSet` with `.get_extra_actions()`.
* Extra actions now set the `url_name` and `url_path` on the decorated method.
* `url_name` is now based on the function name, instead of the `url_path`,
as the path is not always suitable (e.g., capturing arguments in the path).

6448,PullRequestEvent,2017-11-25 14:36:51+00:00,18228948,WattTime/pyiso,3433964,r24mille,6070363,WattTime,154640035,,Fix to historical generation parsing errors on daylight saving time transition days,open,False,182,This branch is based off of @marcparewatttime's work in PR #116. The CAISO `_generation_historical(...)` method had errors parsing data from CAISO's ""Daily Renewables Watch"" report:
* On the 23-hour day transitioning into daylight saving time, parsing the second part of the data file was failing due to the extra erroneous text lines.

* On the 25-hour day transitioning out of daylight saving time, the 1:00 DatetimeIndex value could not be localized due to 1:00 being ambiguous. I've added a 2nd fallback method which assuming DST ""active"" values as those for a daylight savings transition day.
The main commit that fixed the above two issues was 91ea5493a40098c2ee01907af31f594246753bd2.

While in this section of code, I also found an issue with the UTC `start_at` and `end_at` dates being used to request the local ""DailyRenewablesWatch"" URL. This was fixed in 7a9ba0431f9cb5ad18777ec44fa7dc33836e78b6.

6539,PullRequestEvent,2018-02-14 14:42:00+00:00,77478378,ethereum/py-evm,17670147,NIC619,6250754,ethereum,168408793,,Add probe_PAYGAS_mode flag to vm_state.execute_transaction,closed,False,367,"### What was wrong?
Miners need to check if they are getting paid before execute the whole transaction.
Add a mode to test if `PAYGAS` is triggered in the transaction.

This is my first instinct of how to implement this test mode, but there could be better ways to do this instead of adding a flag to `vm_state.execute_transaction`. I was trying not to introduce too many changes.

If `probe_PAYGAS_mode` is on, the message is allocated with `probe_mode_gas_allocated` instead of `transaction.gas` and return immediately once the computation is done. Miner will either tell by `computation.get_PAYGAS_gas_price()` or `OutOfGas` error.
There are a few things needed to be worked out.

- Stop the computation immediately once `PAYGAS` is triggered. By adding a flag to `PAYGAS` function and maybe raise an vm exception once triggered? or create a new way to exit computation for this. But since the intention is only to avoid further computation waste(Because we have to execute the transaction again, for real.) and gas allocated for this test mode is rather low, this might not be necessary.
- The occasion to run this function. We should not run this test mode for the transactions that are already included in a mined block because it's allowed to not paying gas to the miner(`coinbase`).

6586,PullRequestEvent,2016-03-12 00:10:38+00:00,6235174,conda/conda,2797735,mcg1969,6392739,conda,62621314,,Use MatchSpec to verify specs supplied on the command line,open,False,2246,_Copied from #2234_

Without this fix, the steps taken to verify specs supplied on the command line (e.g., `python=3*`) are not as strict as those performed by the solver. So if an invalid spec leaks through to the solver, we get a crash. It's the user's fault, sure, but we're telling the user to file an issue instead of fix their spec. So it's a bug.
This fix uses the solver's own MatchSpec parser to assist in the verification process. Thus if a spec makes it out of this function, we know that the solver will like it later.
Lines 451-455 aren't exercised yet, but will be in master. (And note that if you cherry pick this over there you'll want to resolve the conflict to this version.)

Needed to tune up the `__str__` function for `VersionSpec` to get this to work well.",https://api.github.com/repos/conda/conda/issues/2246,,,2016-03-12T00:10:38Z,1,22,18,3,[],[],[],[],,,,,[],https://github.com/conda/conda/pull/2246.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/conda/conda/pulls/2246/commits

6936,PullRequestEvent,2016-01-19 17:45:30+00:00,18031064,hmmlearn/hmmlearn,185856,superbobry,7037444,hmmlearn,56346472,,ENH: Use typed memoryviews and remove numpy calls in cythonized _viterbi,closed,False,82,"Were using hmmlearn's Viterbi algorithm on large sample sets and wanted to speed it up a bit. On our data (millions of samples) we found a 1000x speedup with these changes. I profiled `TestBaseHMM.test_do_viterbi_pass` in `hmmlearn/tests/test_base.py` and found a 4x speedup in the `_viterbi` function.

The speedups are coming from faster indexing with typed memoryviews and removing calls into python from the inner loops, which would incur a penalty with every iteration. 7021,PullRequestEvent,2016-06-06 20:20:25+00:00,46483999,CiscoUcs/ucsmsdk,4341190,vvb,7289148,CiscoUcs,72771177,,support for multiple parallel commit buffers + ensure latest cookie usage,open,False,56,This PR adds support for using multiple parallel tagged commit buffers. The tag needs to be supplied by the application using the sdk. This could be thread name or anything else. Expectation is one tag name per thread.
add, set, remove and commit now also take a `tag` argument.. This is used to carve out a separate buffer space for this `tag`. Thus we can support multiple parallel transactions now.

This PR also ensures that we always use the latest cookie after a `aaaRefresh` or reLogin. Additionally we also lock `post_elem` to make sure that the driver works serially, while the application works in parallel.",https://api.github.com/repos/CiscoUcs/ucsmsdk/issues/56,,,2016-06-06T20:20:24Z,1,43,13,2,[],[],[],[],,,,,[],https://github.com/CiscoUcs/ucsmsdk/pull/56.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/CiscoUcs/ucsmsdk/pulls/56/commits

7548,PullRequestEvent,2017-11-04 19:29:44+00:00,26876372,dateutil/dateutil,8078968,jbrockmendel,9849410,dateutil,150691218,,Proof of concept _constructor property,open,False,493,"This is meant as a demonstration more than anything else.

There are some attributes/methods of the `parser` class that thematically don't really belong there.  In particular I'm thinking of `_result` and `_build_tzinfo`.  They are currently part of `parser` for subclassing reasons.

The demonstration here is to separate these out into a post-parsing class here called `ParseResult`.  The most important thing here is the `_res_class` property.  It provides a hook for subclassing.  So if downstream someone wants to post-process results differently, they implement a different _resultbase subclass and change the _res_class property.

The pattern could also be applied to allow hooks for `_ymd`, `_timelex`or `parserinfo` (though parserinfo already has a mechanism for passing a custom object).",https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/issues/493,,,2017-11-04T19:29:43Z,2,68,52,1,CONTRIBUTOR,[],[],[],,,,,[],https://github.com/dateutil/dateutil/pull/493.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/pulls/493/commits

7588,PullRequestEvent,2017-10-18 09:13:51+00:00,40975824,common-workflow-language/schema_salad,4450141,tom-tan,9947495,common-workflow-language,147247046,,Better itemize handling for `--print-oneline,open,False,136,"This request fix to show a better message for `--print-oneline` in which it handles itemize better.
Without `--print-oneline`, we see the following messages:
echo.cwl:1:1: Object `echo.cwl` is not valid because
echo.cwl:9:1:     the `outputs` field is not valid because
We expect for `--print-oneline` to show the following messages:
- echo.cwl:14:5: item is invalid because missing required field `id`
- echo.cwl:14:5: item is invalid because invalid field `aa`, expected one of: 'label', 'secondaryFiles', 'streamable', 'doc', 'id', 'outputBinding', 'format', 'type'
echo.cwl:14:5: item is invalid because * missing required field `id` * invalid field `aa`, expected one of: 'label', 'secondaryFiles', 'streamable', 'doc', 'id', 'outputBinding', 'format', 'type'
in which itemize for the same line are accidentally shown in the same line.
echo.cwl:13:5: invalid field `aa`, expected one of: 'label', 'secondaryFiles', 'streamable', 'doc', 'id', 'outputBinding', 'format', 'type'
For better `--print-oneline`, we have to modify `validate.py` to hold the error messages before formatting.
Although it needs much effort, the gain is not so much.

7708,PullRequestEvent,2018-08-03 18:42:18+00:00,112676803,apple/turicreate,1875987,srikris,10639145,apple,205889692,MDExOlB1bGxSZXF1ZXN0MjA1ODg5Njky,Convert empty numpy.ndarray values to array([]) in SArray initializer,closed,False,940,"The C++ backend represents all empty ndarray values as 0-dimensional: it assumes that each element of the shape array is positive. This design is enshrined in our serialization, so we should avoid changing it.

We use `numpy.asarray` on a buffer-protocol wrapper around the backend to reconstitute a `numpy.ndarray` value, but `asarray` does something unexpected when given a zero-dimensional buffer: it creates a one-dimensional, size-1 value read from undefined memory.

Instead, we canonicalize empty backend values to `array([])`. This is consistent with how scalar inputs round-trip to 1-d, size-1 values (e.g., `np.array(3.0)` becomes `np.array([3.0])`). (That behavior is due to the C++ ndarray constructor. Given an empty shape vector, it infers a 1-d shape from the flattened (size-1) data buffer.)

Also, while developing this fix, I realized that my fix in #932 only handled the case for initializing an SArray from a top-level np.matrix. The fix for lists of np.matrix subsumes that fix (which is reverted in this PR): we need to add np.matrix to the types handled by the Cython layer so that np.matrix instances get routed to the code for handling ndarray, not for handling generic sequences.

This pull request fixes several issues:
* Fix #521 . Add validation of dtype strings for ``AttributeSpec`` and ``DatasetSpec``.  This is in part also in response to #520
* ``nwb.misc.yaml`` specified ``dtype: None`` which is not allowed. Assigned appropriate dtype values instead.

* The following spec files contained `` dtype: number`` which is not allowed. Assigned approbriate dtype values instead:``src/pynwb/data/nwb.behavior.yaml``, ``src/pynwb/data/nwb.ecephys.yaml``, ``src/pynwb/data/nwb.icephys.yaml``, ``src/pynwb/data/nwb.image.yaml``
* Several tests used ``dtype='str'`` in the specification of attributes/datasets. Fixed those tests to use ``dtype='text'`` instead.
* Updated the validator to use the data type lists from the new ``DtypeHelper`` class to avoid duplication of code
* Added tests for the ``DtypeHelper`` class and updated constructor behavior of AttributeSpec and DatasetSpec
* Bug fix. ``shape`` constructor argument was not set in ``AttributeSpec``
* Updated ``DtypeSpec.is_ref`` function to also check if a ``DtypeSpec`` defines a reference
* Add some more test cases for the various spec classes

I'm gradually adding in the features needed to recreate the features from [Seko PRB 2017](https://journals.aps.org/prb/abstract/10.1103/PhysRevB.95.144110). This PR includes the tools for the ""elemental representation"" portion of the features:
1. New lookup tables for MagpieData. Some are using data taken from PymatgenData, and duplicated in MagpieData for convenience

3. The ability to compute the covariance between site features
4. The ability to compute skewness and kurtosis of features

8768,PullRequestEvent,2017-12-17 23:55:24+00:00,26516210,certbot/certbot,5235109,joohoi,17889013,certbot,158810617,,Interfaces for TLS updates and plugin specific updates,open,False,5334,"This PR is based on the design proposal by @bmw
Two new interfaces: `ServerTLSUpdater` and `InstallerSpecificUpdater` are being introduced.

`ServerTLSUpdater` allows updating the TLS configuration using the selected installer plugin. This provides way for us to keep the user configuration secure if and when new vulnerabilities in TLS configuration are being discovered.
""""""Set the server's TLS config to latest recommended version.
This function will only be called if the user hasn't disabled TLS
:param str domain: domain in the lineage being updated
:param bool renewed: if the certificate was renewed on this run
:param lineage: Certificate lineage object that is defined if the
The installer will also need to register the interface by using method
`interfaces.ServerTLSUpdater.register(InstallerPluginClass)`  in the end of the module of installer class.

The method `server_tls_updates` will be called on every time Certbot is run with `renew` verb, and will have the parameters `renewed` and `lineage` set when the lineage has been renewed on this invocation.
`InstallerSpecificUpdater` provides the functionality needed to deploy security enhancements in dynamic manner. Few examples being prefetching OCSP responses and gradually increasing HSTS max-age.
Implementing `InstallerSpecificUpdater` involves installer providing the following methods:
""""""Perform any update types defined by the installer.
If an installer is a subclass of the class containing this method, this
function will always be called when ""certbot renew"" is run. If the
update defined by the installer should be run conditionally, the
installer needs to handle checking the conditions itself.
:param str domain: domain in the lineage being updated
:param bool renewed: if the certificate was renewed on this run
:param lineage: Certificate lineage object that is defined if the
""""""Verify that there is configuration errors in handling the installer
specific updates that would be run.
If an installer is a subclass of the class containing this method, this
function will always be called when ""certbot renew"" is run.
:param str domain: domain in the lineage being updated
The installer will also need to register the interface by using method
`interfaces.InstallerSpecificUpdater.register(InstallerPluginClass)` in the end of the module of installer class.
The method `installer_specific_updates` works exactly as the `server_tls_updates` for `ServerTLSUpdater`, described above.

`verify_installer_specific_updates` provides the installer a way to verify that the configuration is sane for the requested enhancements. Ie. NOT having `--disable-installer-updates` and an parameter requesting an enhancement that needs dynamic updates defined at the same time. If there is a configuration problem, `errors.MisconfigurationError` should be raised.",https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/issues/5334,,,2017-12-17T23:55:24Z,2,336,6,8,MEMBER,[],[],[],,,,,[],https://github.com/certbot/certbot/pull/5334.patch,https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/pulls/5334/commits

9381,PullRequestEvent,2018-01-19 21:55:38+00:00,18765629,openvax/pyensembl,48441,iskandr,33269729,openvax,163550235,,Fixing travis,closed,False,196,"Problem: changes to Travis seem to have limited us to <4gb / 19gb of disk space. Our unit tests use significantly more than that while installing multiple Ensembl releases.
* Tell Travis to install fewer things using `language: minimal` in `.travis.yml`.

* Don't index every table on `['seqname', 'start', 'end', 'strand']`, since we're already doing `['seqname', 'start', 'end']` and if there are query results on both strands they can probably be efficiently filtered.
* Got rid of `GTF` object, which existed to cache query results on disk as CSV files. This slightly improved long-term performance at the cost of massive disk usage.

During my hunt for disk savings I also added a `delete-source-files` command to the `pyensembl` CLI which allows you delete `GTF` and `FASTA` files while leaving their indexed representations in place.
